railcard.note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

rules.availability: Available for purchase up to 1-year in advance of travel, but you must provide evidence of valid travel to the Continent via rail or sea, at the time of purchase.
rules.bookingDeadlines: You must hold a ticket or confirmation of cross-border travel
rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: Customers who change the date of their international travel leg from London, must request the ticket to be re-issued from the original retailer with a new date of travel (no earlier than the day before onward travel). You may need to pay a difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the date and time you need to travel.

validity.dayOutward: Tickets are valid for travel any day between the date shown on the ticket and for two calendar months. Travel must be completed no earlier than the date before cross-border travel, and no later than 04:29 the date following the expiry date shown. e.g. Euro Apex CIV (Single) valid from 01/03/2025 to 31/05/2025 may be used on any intermediate date, providing cross-border travel is on the same day or day following, but travel must be completed no later than 04:29 on 01/06/2025

rules.changesToTravelPlans: A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, or change the date of travel. Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey. NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative Anytime product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.

name: Evening Day Return (West Midlands Railway)
description: Off-Peak fares are cheaper tickets for travelling on trains that are less busy. You may be required to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route.

validity.dayOutward: Conditions of Use. You must travel in the class of accommodation shown on your ticket. Where accommodation is provided, you may upgrade to First Class. The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket. When and where the ticket can be used. Off-Peak tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. Evening Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and from 18:00 until 0429 on the following day, by which time all travel must be completed. Time restrictions are indicated by a restriction shown against the ticket's Restriction Code. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
validity.dayReturn: Conditions of Use. You must travel in the class of accommodation shown on your ticket. Where accommodation is provided, you may upgrade to First Class. name: Evening Out Day Travelcard
description: Evening Out Day Travelcard is available on shorter distance journeys to London, Monday - Saturday. It allows customers to travel to London and enjoy unlimited travel throughout London on National Rail, London Underground, Docklands Light Railway, Tramlink and London Bus services within Fare Zones 1-6

validity.dayOutward: Evening Out Day Travelcard tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
validity.dayReturn: Evening Out Day Travelcard tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
validity.timeOutward: The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. The times when you may use your Evening Out Day Travelcard ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection. The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code]. You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable. If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).

rules.conditions: Evening Out Sigle tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Evening Out ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned. In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare. If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel .

name: Fixed Family Single (First Class)
description: Fixed Family Advance tickets are single (one-way) tickets for many longer and some shorter distance journeys, are available in First Class and Standard Class and can offer the best available price for each journey. These tickets are for groups of up to six consisting of a minimum of one adult and one child and up to two adults and up to four children. rules.conditions: Change of Journey/Excess Fare paid Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original Advance ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of Advance ticket, Excess Fare paid and any administration fees paid, the Advance ticket and change of journey fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £10 per group ticket. If your journey is cancelled or disrupted If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate them on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed, and as a result you decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets and you will not be charged an administration fee. There may be rare occasions where a cheaper Advance fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.

The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same. The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable. An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket (per group) for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded. name: Flexi Season Ticket
description: A Flexi Season ticket provides customers with 8 days of travel in 28 days - any time between two named stations. The 8 days of travel doesn't need to be specified in advance, giving part-time commuters more flexibility to travel when they want and need. The Flexi Season ticket will offer a minimum of 20% discount on an equivalent Monthly Season ticket, offering savings for commuters travelling during peak times, 2-3 days a week. Flexi Season tickets will be available as either a Smartcard or Barcode ticket, check with your train operator to see what they offer. If you don't have a Smartcard then please allow plenty of time to get one before you travel - they can take up to 5 days to arrive by post.

rules.changesToTravelPlans: It is not possible to change your journey. Any outstanding days' worth of travel should be refunded and a new Flexi Season Ticket purchased.

name: Freedom of Devon & Cornwall Rover
description: Rail Rover for unlimited travel (subject to time restrictions shown) within Devon and Cornwall. Available for flexible periods, either 3 days within 7 [D07] or 8 days within 15 [D37]. Further information can be found on Great Western Railway's website.

validity.dayOutward: Valid for 3 days out of 7 or 8 days out of 15 according to choice. The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting. Except on Sleeper services, this ticket is valid for travel until 04:29 of the morning immediately following the expiry date shown on the ticket. All travel must be completed by this time. Sleeper Services towards London Only valid on the date shown on the ticket (within the area covered by the ticket) Sleeper Services towards Penzance (Saturday Morning Arrivals only) For Sleeper services arriving on a Saturday morning only , this ticket must be valid on the date of arrival at your destination but need not be valid on the date of departure of the Sleeper service. The date of arrival is the first date of validity of the ticket. (Only valid within the area covered by the ticket.)
validity.dayReturn: Valid for 3 days out of 7 or 8 days out of 15 according to choice. The ticket-holder must validate the ticket for each day's travel by writing the date in the next box on the card. This must be done in ink before starting. Except on Sleeper services, this ticket is valid for travel until 04:29 of the morning immediately following the expiry date shown on the ticket. All travel must be completed by this time. Sleeper Services towards London Only valid on the date shown on the ticket (within the area covered by the ticket) Sleeper Services towards Penzance (Saturday Morning Arrivals only) For Sleeper services arriving on a Saturday morning only , this ticket must be valid on the date of arrival at your destination but need not be valid on the date of departure of the Sleeper service. The date of arrival is the first date of validity of the ticket. (Only valid within the area covered by the ticket.)
validity.timeOutward: Valid from 08:45 Monday to Friday, with easements on earlier services as follows: All day on the St Ives Branch All day on trains from Exeter to Barnstaple or Okehampton 08:35 from Barnstaple and 08:43 from Umberleigh, on the service towards Exeter 08:30 from Newton Abbot and 08:42 from Totnes, on the service towards Plymouth 08:38 service from Newton Abbot to Paignton 08:09 service from Plymouth to Penzance 08:28 service from Plymouth to Gunnislake 08:16 service from Liskeard to Looe 07:25, 08:24 Exeter St Davids - Axminster Valid at any time on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holidays.

rules.availability: Available any day.
rules.retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
rules.bookingDeadlines: Rover tickets may not be issued more than 3 days in advance of their validity (5 days for telesales and internet bookings).
rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: Refunds are only available on unused tickets surrendered before the day of travel and may be subject to an administration fee. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

name: Friends and Family Ticket (Network West Midlands)
description: nNetwork Friends and Family fare is available off-peak for one adult for £1 and up to two children aged 5-15 for 50p each when accompanying a holder of a valid adult nTrain or nNetwork Season Ticket , for a return journey between two stations in the Network West Midlands area, as long as the stations are in the same rail zones covered by the nTrain or nNetwork Season Ticket . All persons using this fare, including the Season Ticket holder, must travel together at all times. Friends and Family fares are available between any two stations in the Network West Midlands area (plus Rugeley Trent Valley, Rugeley Town, Hednesford, Cannock and Landywood - only if a train Season ticket is held) on journeys covered by the Season Ticket, e.g. if Season Ticket is valid for zones 1-3 then the Friends and Family fare is also valid for journeys in zone 1-3. Season ticket holders must possess a 1 week, 4 week, monthly Direct Debit, 13 week or 52 week nTrain or nNetwork Season Ticket . Full details of the full range of tickets, periods of validity, zones and maps can be found on the nNetwork [Network West Midlands] website .

validity.dayOutward: Valid for travel only on the date on the ticket and on the routes permitted by the accompanying passenger's Season Ticket.
validity.dayReturn: Valid for travel only on the date on the ticket and on the routes permitted by the accompanying passenger's Season Ticket.
validity.timeOutward: Friends and Family fares are available for off-peak travel after 09:30 on Monday to Fridays and anytime on Saturdays, Sundays and Bank Holiday Mondays.

Not available with any other rail offer. No further discounts for Railcard holders. Not valid to or from stations outside the nNetwork area .

rules.availability: Available from ticket offices at all railway stations.
rules.bookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: Refunds are available on completely unused tickets, within 28 days of the expiry date, less a £10 admin fee, per booking. If some travel has been completed, the refund should be calculated as follows: Calculate the price that would have been paid if the customer had purchased Anytime tickets for all members of the group, for all travel completed using the ticket. Add one £10 admin fee to this total. Subtract this amount from the total price paid for the tickets. The remaining amount (if any) is the refund due. (In some circumstances no refund will be due.) For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.
rules.specialConditions: Tickets are only available from the origin to Gatwick Airport (not available with Gatwick as the origin). They are only valid via the North Downs route (Reading – Guildford – Redhill – Gatwick Airport) and are not valid for travel via London. Customers using a Gatwick Flyaway ticket must travel together at all times during the outward and return journey. Proof of airline travel may be requested when purchasing tickets. One ticket covers the whole adult group. Children are issued with separate individual tickets. Tickets are currently only available from ticket offices, so will currently only be issued on CCST / paper tickets.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. There are no time restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket.

If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased. The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same. The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable. An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded. Please note Where a GC Advance ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.

name: GWR Promotional Advance Fares (Standard Class)
description: GWR Promotional Advance tickets are Single (one-way) tickets for many longer and some shorter distance journeys, are available in First Class and Standard Class where appropriate. These tickets are valid only on the specific booked train(s), and are non-refundable except under the terms of the National Rail Conditions of Travel. They can be changed (for a fee) for alternate dates / times subject to availability.

validity.dayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
validity.dayReturn: Not applicable.
validity.timeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
validity.timeReturn: Not applicable.

rules.availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
rules.retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents. available at GWR.com/sale and GWR App.
rules.bookingDeadlines: Subject to availability and may not be available at certain times. Not valid with other offers, promotions or discounts. Selected routes only. Full terms and conditions at GWR.com/sale
rules.refunds: Advance tickets are non-refundable, except under the terms of the National Rail Conditions of Travel. Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original Advance ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of Advance ticket plus Excess Fare plus £10 fee, the Advance ticket and £10 fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the excess fare paid, less a £10 refund fee. If the train the customer purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate them on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train the customer purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed and as a result, customers decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets and they will not be charged an administration fee. There may be rare occasions where a cheaper Advance fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. Customers will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change. Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased. The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same. The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable, plus a £10 administration fee per person, per single ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.

There may be rare occasions where a cheaper Advance fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. Customers will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change. Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased. The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same. The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable, plus a £10 administration fee per person, per single ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.

rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable. If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).

Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

name: Lancashire Day Ranger
description: A day ranger ticket, allowing unlimited travel in the ranger area which covers most of Lancashire and a little further afield. View the map on Northern website .

validity.dayOutward: Valid on the date shown on the ticket.
validity.dayReturn: Valid on the date shown on the ticket.
validity.timeOutward: Valid after 08:45 Monday to Friday. Valid any time at weekends and on Bank Holidays.
validity.timeReturn: Valid after 08:45 Monday to Friday. Valid any time at weekends and on Bank Holidays.

railcard.note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

rules.availability: Any day.
rules.retailing: Any staffed railway station. Can also be purchased from the conductor on the train within the validity area when boarding at an unstaffed station or if the ticket office is closed.
rules.bookingDeadlines: None.
rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: Refundable only if surrendered before the start date. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

name: Lincoln Business Package (LNER)
description: Standard Class Return ticket to London with added benefits. The package includes: Standard Class Return rail travel on London North Eastern Railway services. One outward and one return free seat reservation. A £5 complimentary catering voucher, which can be used on the either the outward or return journey. Overnight parking at Lincoln Central Multi-story Car Park - Melville St, Lincoln LN5 7BP (two consecutive days only). If a longer stay is required this will need to be paid for when exiting at the car park kiosk.

validity.dayOutward: Valid any day, as dated on the ticket.
validity.dayReturn: Valid any day within one calendar month.
validity.timeOutward: Valid for the train specified on the ticket.
validity.timeReturn: Valid on any London North Eastern Railway service.

rules.availability: The product will be offered to select corporates through corporate channels, following prior agreement with London North Eastern Railway.
rules.retailing: Available to selected corporate clients, booking through corporate channels. Sales channels include third-party travel agents and the LNER4Business corporate travel website. Not available online or at stations
rules.bookingDeadlines: Can be bought up to 23.59 on the day before travel, subject to availability.
rules.refunds: Completely unused and returned complete - full refund less any local cancellation charge. Unused outward leg and partially completed journeys - no refund. Unused return legs - from the ticket value, deduct the equivalent Standard Class Anytime Single fare. Deduct a further £20·00 plus any local cancellation fees, and refund the remainder. Car parking used but ticket unused - deduct £20 plus any local cancellation fees and refund the remainder. Buffet voucher not used - no refund.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: Change of outward date allowed, without penalty, if done in advance of travel date and within the validity of the ticket. Change of outward train allowed, on the date shown on the ticket, without penalty. Upgrade to First Class is allowed upon payment of the difference between the SOS and FOS fare for each leg excessed to/from London. No additional benefits are available

name: Lincolnshire Day Ranger
description: Standard Class multi-journey off-peak day ticket

validity.dayOutward: Valid in the area bounded by Doncaster, Lincoln, Newark, Nottingham, Grantham, Peterborough, Spalding and Sleaford. Also includes Peterborough-Stamford, Lincoln-Doncaster via Gainsborough and Lincoln-Cleethorpes via Barnetby after 0900.
validity.dayReturn: Valid in the area bounded by Doncaster, Lincoln, Newark, Nottingham, Grantham, Peterborough, Spalding and Sleaford. Also includes Peterborough-Stamford, Lincoln-Doncaster via Gainsborough and Lincoln-Cleethorpes via Barnetby after 0900.

railcard.note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

groupTravel.note: Child discounts. Railcard discounts for holders of Senior, 16-25 and Disabled Persons Railcards. Discounts are also available to holders of Staff Travel and New Entrant Cards.

rules.availability: Any day.
rules.retailing: Ticket offices, travel centres,.
rules.bookingDeadlines: No advance purchase necessary.
rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: Refunds are only given on Rail Rover tickets returned before the start date shown on them and are subject to an administration charge. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

name: LNER ADVANCE ITX
description: Advance tickets are available on many longer and some shorter distance journeys, they are available in First Class and Standard Class and offer the best available price for each journey. Tickets must be booked in advance of travel and are subject to availability. Advance tickets are valid only on the specific booked train but can be changed before departure of the first reserved service. Advance ITX Fares are used by agents.

validity.dayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
validity.dayReturn: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
validity.timeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
validity.timeReturn: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.

rules.availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
rules.retailing: Train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.
rules.bookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability. Generally, the earlier the ticket is booked the better the deal. Tickets are available up to 12 weeks before travel.
rules.refunds: Advance tickets are non-refundable. * * * * * * * * * * * * Change of Journey/Excess Fare paid Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original Advance ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of Advance ticket, Excess Fare paid and any administration fees paid, the Advance ticket and change of journey fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £10. If your journey is cancelled or disrupted If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate them on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed, and as a result you decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets and you will not be charged an administration fee.There may be rare occasions where a cheaper Advance fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances Advance tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. You will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when they request a change. Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased. The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same. The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable. An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded. Please note: Where an Advance ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.

name: LNER Carnet 5 (Standard Class)
description: A book of Standard Class Return tickets for 5 journeys

validity.dayOutward: Carnet are valid any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
validity.dayReturn: Carnet are valid any day within 3 months of the date on the ticket.
validity.timeOutward: Valid on any train.
validity.timeReturn: Valid on any train.

rules.conditions: Generally the same as 'Anytime tickets', except that tickets are valid for three months, but are not refundable. Route and/or Train company validity is as shown on the ticket routeing. Can be bought at any time up to departure but cannot be bought on board trains.

name: LNER Corporate Return (Standard Class)
description: Mandatory reservation required on the outward journey, however the return leg can be flexible (to within one calendar month, if required), though reservations are recommended. This advance purchase product is available to selected corporate clients and only sold through corporate retail channels. It provides Standard Class rail travel on-board London North Eastern Railway services and is provided with a compulsory seat reservation. Not valid for travel with any other operators.

validity.dayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
validity.dayReturn: Flexible (to within one calendar month, if required).
validity.timeOutward: Valid only on the London North Eastern Railway service shown on the ticket You must be at the departure station shown on the ticket in good time to catch the train. If you miss the first train on which you are booked for any reason, a new ticket must be purchased. If delays occur while travelling, you will be allowed to take the next available train(s) to complete your journey.
validity.timeReturn: No time restrictions apply.

rules.conditions: This is to be used to offer Standard Class corporate route deals to selected corporate clients and should only be sold alongside a London North Eastern Railway corporate discount. Not to be sold to any corporate customers without prior agreement with London North Eastern Railway. No other child, railcard, or group discounts permitted.
rules.availability: This product is intended for corporate customers who have strict limitations on class of travel as part of their corporate travel policies. The product will be offered to select corporates through corporate channels, following prior agreement with London North Eastern Railway.
rules.retailing: Available to selected corporate clients, booking through corporate channels and sold using an agreed corporate discount. Sales channels include third-party travel agents and the LNER4Business corporate travel website. Not available online or at stations.
rules.bookingDeadlines: Can be bought up to 23.59 on the day before travel, subject to availability.
rules.refunds: Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused to the retailer within 28 days of the ticket expiry date of the ticket. You may be required to pay an administration fee. The refund amount will take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: Changes to travel plan are allowed, without penalty, on or until the date of travel. Where the outward date and time of travel is changed, the ticket should be re-issued by the retailer showing the new date and or time of travel.

name: LNER Mid-Week First Upgrade
description: Supplement to upgrade a Standard Class ticket to First Class on London North Eastern Railway at OFF-PEAK Mid-week periods. The cost of a London North Eastern Railway Mid-Week Upgrade varies depending on the journey: Between London/Stevenage and Doncaster/Selby/Brough/Hull or any stations in between - £39 per single journey. Between London/Stevenage and York/Wakefield or any stations further north - £49 per single journey. For LNER journeys wholly north of Stevenage - £39 per single journey.

name: London Evening Out
description: A semi-flexible day return from selected Greater Anglia station to Stratford and London Liverpool Street. Available in First Class (OFK) and Standard Class (OSK) versions.

validity.dayOutward: Booked train only
validity.dayReturn: Same day as outward travel
validity.timeOutward: Available on trains departing after 13:59
validity.timeReturn: Any service departing after 19:04

rules.bookingDeadlines: Book by 23:59 on the day before travel.
rules.refunds: Refunds are not available.

name: London Night Out
description: A semi-flexible overnight return ticket to London and Stratford (London) from stations on the Great Eastern Main Line (Norwich to Colchester) and branches. Travel outwards in the afternoon or evening towards London on a specified train and return the following day on any train departing before 12.01 except between 06.59 and 08.01. If the return day is a Saturday, Sunday or Bank Holiday, return at any time before 12.01. Tickets are available in First Class (OFL) and Standard Class (OSL) versions.

validity.dayOutward: Valid on first day of validity only.
validity.dayReturn: Valid to return on the day after the outward journey only.
validity.timeOutward: Valid on the booked train only. Available trains are from early afternoon onwards. The exact time of the first valid train varies from station to station. Journey planners will show when the ticket is available.
validity.timeReturn: Valid on any train scheduled to depart before 06.59 and after 08.01, but before 12.01 (any time before 12.01 if the return journey is on a Saturday, Sunday or Bank Holiday).

name: London North Eastern Railway Family Return
description: The London North Eastern Railway (LNER) Family Return provides a simple to understand return-journey price for a small group of up to two adults and four children, to travel onboard LNER services only. The product is sold subject to availability, on a first-come, first-served basis, and seats are reserved for the whole party for both the outward and return journey.

validity.dayOutward: Long Weekend Return tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket which must be either a Friday (WKD) or Saturday (WKE).
validity.dayReturn: Long Weekend Return tickets are valid for return travel Mondays only. (This includes travel up until 04:29 on Tuesday.) Not valid for use on Sundays

rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: - For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.

rules.availability: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel.
rules.retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
rules.bookingDeadlines: None.
rules.refunds: Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable. If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).

name: Low Saver Return (CIV)
description: A Standard Class return ticket for travel on Train and Ferry which can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.

validity.dayOutward: The outward journey must be on the date shown on the ticket. This type of ticket may have restrictions on the time of day that you can travel. These are generally in the early morning, but may also be in the early evening.
validity.dayReturn: The return journey must take place within one calendar month(s) of the date shown on the ticket. This type of ticket may have restrictions on the time of day that you can travel. These are generally in the early morning, but may also be in the early evening.

railcard.note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: Disabled Persons Railcard, Veterans Railcard, Family & Friends Railcard, HM Forces Railcard, Senior Railcard, Two Together Railcard, 16-25 Railcard, or 26-30 Railcard. 50% discount for 16-17 Saver. See terms & conditions of individual Railcard for time restrictions.

groupTravel.note: Children (Aged 5 to 15 inclusive): 50% discount.Up to two children under five can travel free with each fare paying passenger. rules.bookingDeadlines: This ticket can be purchased in advance or immediately before travel.
rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: A full refund is available if you return the completely unused ticket to the place of purchase within 28 days of expiry of the ticket. The amount of the refund will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: You may change your travel plans at any time within the validity of the ticket without having to pay an additional fare. Restrictions on when you can travel with this ticket may apply to both the outward and return journeys - please check before travelling.

name: LumoFixed (Standard Class)
description: One-way Standard Class ticket offering the best available price for each journey. Non-refundable. Change of journeys, available up to the day before travel, may incur a fee (See ‘Changes’ section below). Tickets must be booked in advance of travel and are subject to availability. Valid only on the specific booked train. Luggage: Permitted luggage onboard is 1 suitcase of max dimensions 63cm x 41cm x 27cm and a small bag which can fit under the seat or on an overhead rack or lap. See T&Cs for full details. https://www.lumo.co.uk/plan-your-journey/onboard-luggage-policy

validity.dayOutward: Valid only on the date shown on the ticket.
validity.dayReturn: N/A
validity.timeOutward: Valid only at the specified time and train printed on the ticket.
validity.timeReturn: N/A

railcard.note: 1/3 off with the following Railcards: 16-25, 26-30, Family & Friends, Senior, HM Forces, Disabled Persons, Two Together Railcards.

rules.conditions: Where an excess fare has been paid up to the price of a walk-up ticket, the excess fare paid varies the terms under which the passenger can travel, but it does not alter the refund terms of the original LumoFixed ticket. If the passenger subsequently asks for a refund on the combination of LumoFixed ticket, Excess Fare paid and any administration fees paid, the LumoFixed ticket and change of journey fee remain non-refundable and a refund is only payable on the difference in fare paid. This may be subject to a refund fee of no more than £10 if your journey is cancelled or disrupted. If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed by more than 60 minutes, special arrangements will be made to accommodate you on another train (although a seat cannot be guaranteed). If the train you purchased a ticket for is cancelled or delayed, and as a result you decide not to travel, a refund will be offered on completely unused tickets, and you will not be charged an administration fee. There may be rare occasions where a cheaper LumoFixed fare becomes available closer to the time of travel, for example if a group booking is cancelled and extra seats become available. Please note that in these circumstances LumoFixed tickets that have already been bought cannot be refunded in part or in full.
rules.availability: Tickets are valid only on the date and train service(s) shown on the ticket(s). Where applicable, valid only for travel in the class and reserved seat(s) shown on the ticket(s).
rules.retailing: Any National Rail staffed ticket office, train company telesales office, online at most train company website and selected rail appointed travel agents.
rules.bookingDeadlines: Tickets must be purchased in advance of travel, subject to availability. Generally, the earlier the ticket is booked the better the deal. Tickets are available up to 24 weeks before travel.
rules.refunds: LumoFixed tickets are non-refundable
rules.changesToTravelPlans: Changes to time or date of travel must be arranged the day before departure of the first reserved train printed on the ticket, after which the ticket has no value and a new one must be purchased. You will need to present the ticket(s) and reservation(s) when a change is requested. Changes to tickets cannot be made on-board the train. If customers board a train without a ticket and reservation for that service, a new ticket must be purchased. Changes can be applied until the day before travel, NOT on the day. The origin, destination and Train Company or route shown on the ticket(s) must remain the same. The difference between the price paid and cost of the next suitable fare for the journey is payable. An administration fee of no more than £10 may be charged per ticket for each change to a journey. If customers change to a train on which a cheaper fare is available, the difference will not be refunded. Please note: Where a LumoFixed ticket has been purchased online and the ticket has either been self-printed at home or downloaded to a mobile device this may alter the rules for changing the ticket. The relevant conditions will be made clear when the ticket is bought.

name: Luton Airport DUO Return
description: The Luton Airport DUO Return is available on selected journeys to and from Luton Airport and gives a 25% discount * on the train journey between your station and Luton Airport Parkway for two Adults travelling together at Off-Peak times, compared to the price of two Standard Class Off-Peak Return tickets for the same journey. Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are only valid in Standard Class and when both people travel together, they're not valid if you travel on different services. The times when you can use your Luton Airport DUO Return ticket will depend on the journey you're making and you will be advised when buying your ticket. The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are not available for travel in First Class. * The 25% discount applies to the rail journey to and from Luton Airport Parkway railway station, there is no discount on the price of the journey on the Luton DART between Luton Airport Parkway railway station and Luton Airport.

validity.dayOutward: Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are valid for outward travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following morning. If you cannot complete your journey on the same day you will be allowed to finish it the following day - see 'Break of journey' below. Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are only valid when both people travel together, they're not valid if you travel on different services.
validity.dayReturn: Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are valid for return travel within one calendar month from the start date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 in the morning after last day of validity. Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are only valid when both people travel together, they're not valid if you travel on different services.
validity.timeOutward: The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. The times when you may use your Luton Airport DUO Return ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection. The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code]. You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
validity.timeReturn: The National Rail Journey Planner will automatically work out which tickets are valid for your journey. The times when you may use your Luton Airport DUO Return ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection. The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code]. You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

railcard.note: There are no additional discounts for Railcard holders on Luton Airport DUO Return tickets.

groupTravel.note: There are no additional discounts for larger groups on Luton Airport DUO Return tickets.

rules.conditions: The Luton Airport DUO Return is available on selected journeys to and from Luton Airport and gives a 25% discount* for two Adults travelling together at Off-Peak times, compared to the price of two Off-Peak Return tickets for the same journey. Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are only valid in Standard Class and when both people travel together, they're not valid if you travel on different services. Luton Airport DUO Return tickets are not available for travel in First Class. The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket. Luton Airport DUO Return tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Luton Airport DUO Return ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned. In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare. If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Liverpool Street and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel . * The 25% discount applies to the rail journey to and from Luton Airport Parkway railway station, there is no discount on the price of the journey on the Luton DART between Luton Airport Parkway railway station and Luton Airport.
rules.availability: Luton Airport DUO Return tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel. The outward part of the ticket is valid on date shown on ticket. Return travel within one calendar month. There are no child fares or discounts available for Railcard holders, Annual Gold Card holders or holders of other discount cards.
rules.retailing: Luton Airport DUO Return tickets can be sold at Ticket Offices and Travel Centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
rules.bookingDeadlines: None.

If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the Luton Airport DUO Return ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable. If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).

name: Merseyrail Railpass
description: Zonal Season Ticket for trains throughout Merseyside and beyond. Railpass is a season ticket for regular travel on trains within Merseyside (and beyond towards Chester, Ellesmere Port and Ormskirk). The area is split into a number of zones and areas, so a Railpass is sold for the specific zones or areas through which the regular journey is taken. Railpass is valid for travel at any time of the day and on any day of the week for an unlimited number of journeys within the zones and expiry date on the ticket. name: nTrain Season Ticket (Network West Midlands)
description: Season Ticket for the 'network' (Network West Midlands PTE area ). ntrain is a Season Ticket which allows unlimited rail travel (only) in the zone or zones in which it is valid. Tickets can be bought in the following combination of zones: Zone 1 Zone 1 & 2 Zone 1, 2 & 3 Zone 1, 2, 3 & 4 Zone 1, 2, 3, 4, & 5 [ all zones ] Zone 2, 3, 4 & 5 Tickets that cover Zone 5 can also be used beyond Bloxwich North to all stations to Rugeley Trent Valley. Tickets can be bought for periods 7 days, 4 weeks, 1 month and then any period up to one year. nTrain Season Tickets are not valid on Metro or bus services. Full details of the full range of tickets, periods of validity, zones and maps can be found on the Network West Midlands website .

rules.refunds: You can apply for a refund on a nTrain Season Ticket valid for seven days or more as long as your ticket still has at least seven days validity remaining or three days for seven day season tickets. The outstanding validity of the ticket will be refunded and is calculated from the date we receive the ticket less a £10 administration fee. To get a refund please follow the instructions below. Please note you will need to supply your original ticket not a copy: - For tickets bought at the station you can apply for a refund at your local station - For tickets bought online, log on to your online account fill in the relevant form - For tickets bought online at www.networkwestmidlands.com or via the Corporate Sales Scheme can write to: Direct Sales, Centro House, 16 Summer Lane, Birmingham B19 3SD. - For tickets bought through the Direct Debit Scheme refunds are not available, you must give at least one months notice by telephone, email or in writing of your intention to cease Direct Debit payments. For further details call 0121 214 7550.

rules.conditions: The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket. This ticket may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned. In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel
rules.retailing: Online, through the South Western Railway website or app, and other online retailers. Also available at ticket offices.
rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £10 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable. If you wish to change the date of travel, or the origin or destination of your ticket, it may be necessary to buy a new ticket and apply for a refund on your existing ticket (see Refund Policy).

name: Off-Peak Carnet (Thameslink and Great Northern)
description: Please note Flexi Season tickets have replaced our Peak (Anytime) Carnet tickets. Off-Peak Carnet tickets offer a 10% discount on Standard Class travel, compared to the price of Off-Peak Day Return fares between the following stations and London: Cambridge* - Royston* - Huntingdon* - St Neots* - Welwyn Garden City* - Hatfield* - Potters Bar* - Hertford North* - Bedford** - Luton Airport Parkway** - Harpenden** - St Albans City** You can buy a Off-Peak Carnet book of either 5 or 10 single journey tickets. Your Off-Peak Carnet tickets will need to be used within three months. Off-Peak Carnet tickets are one-way, so you will need a separate book of Off-Peak Carnet tickets for your return journey. You cannot combine your 10% discount with Railcard or GroupSave discounts. No First Class, Off-Peak Carnet tickets are available. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ *From these stations travel is available to / from the following London stations: London Kings Cross - London St Pancras International - Old Street - Moorgate **From these stations travel is available to / from the following London stations: London St Pancras - Farringdon - City Thameslink - London Blackfriars - London Bridge.

Tickets to the following destinations cannot be used on trains leaving London between 16:30 and 19:00: Cambridge* - Royston* - Huntingdon* - St Neots* - Bedford** - Luton Airport Parkway** - Harpenden** - St Albans City**

rules.conditions: The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket. Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned. In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare. If the route shown on the ticket has a + or † symbol then the ticket includes the cost of travelling between certain London Stations (e.g. between Victoria and Paddington) by either London Underground, Docklands Light Railway (DLR) or Thameslink services as appropriate to the route of the journey being made. Tickets will only be accepted by London Underground and DLR up to the last day of validity of the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. Other than to change trains, a break of journey at intermediate London Underground or DLR stations is not permitted. All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel .

rules.retailing: Ticket offices and travel centres, self-service ticket machines at stations, rail-appointed travel agents, online at most train company websites or through other online retailers or train company telesales offices.
rules.bookingDeadlines: Tickets can be bought in advance or immediately before travel. You can reserve a seat on many longer distance services. Reservations are recommended on busy services. If you wish to change your time of travel you can pay the difference between the cost of the ticket held and the cost of the ticket that is most appropriate for the journey you need to make. In many cases, if you are still travelling at a time when your ticket is not restricted, no additional fare is payable. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. rules.changesToTravelPlans: A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel. Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey. NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.

rules.bookingDeadlines: None.
rules.refunds: For tickets purchased until 31 March 2026: Your ticket is refundable. If you decide not to use your ticket to make all or part of your intended journey then you can get a refund by returning your unused ticket to the ticket office or place of purchase (for tickets bought via websites, telesales or travel agents) within 28 days of the ticket expiry date. You may be required to pay an administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket). The refund amount will normally take into account any use you have made of the ticket and in some circumstances no refund will be paid. For tickets purchased from 1 April 2026: Refundable until 23:59 the day before the ticket becomes valid for travel. Not refundable once the ticket has become valid for travel unless Condition 30 of the National Rail Conditions of Travel (failure of train service) applies. In exceptional circumstances a refund may be considered on unused or part-used tickets that have become valid for travel. In such cases an application must be made to the retailer within 28 days of the expiry date of the ticket and should include the reason for non-use. Additional evidence may be requested, refunds will be on a discretionary basis, and any use made of the ticket may mean that in some circumstances no refund will be due. Refunds are only available from the retailer that sold the tickets. An administration fee (up to a maximum of £5 per ticket) may be applied.
rules.changesToTravelPlans: A ticket will need to be changed in order to travel to a destination further than where it was purchased to, change the route of the ticket, change the class of travel from Standard Class to First Class, change the date of travel, or change the time you are allowed to travel. Changes to tickets purchased online should be made by the retailer from which the ticket was bought. Staffed station ticket offices can also make changes in most cases, provided that this is done before the start of the journey. NOTE: A change to the date of travel can only be made BEFORE the date on which the ticket becomes valid for travel. Other alterations such as changes to the route or class of travel will be subject to a suitable alternative product being available. In some cases, it may be cheaper to purchase a new ticket.

name: Off-Peak Family Travelcard
description: An Off-Peak Family Travelcard allows a minimum of 1 Adult and 1 Child (5-15 years old inclusive) return travel to London and enjoy unlimited travel throughout London on National Rail, London Underground, DLR, London Trams, and London Bus services within Zones 1-6. All group members must travel together at all times.

validity.dayOutward: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
validity.dayReturn: Off-Peak Day Return tickets are valid for travel on the date shown on the ticket and until 04:29 the following day. All travel must be completed by this time.
validity.timeOutward: The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection. The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code]. You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.
validity.timeReturn: The times when you may use your Off-Peak ticket are indicated by a Restriction Code applicable to the journey you are making. This can be viewed by clicking the link from the Journey Planner ticket terms and conditions page once you have made your selection. The Restriction Code is a two character (alpha-numeric) code. This is shown on most tickets and the meaning can be found by going to www.nationalrail.co.uk/XX [where 'XX' is the two character code]. You will also be advised of the restriction when buying your ticket.

rules.conditions: The outward part of the ticket is only valid for travel when accompanied by the unused return part of the same ticket. Eligibility Off-Peak Family Travelcard is valid only when at least 1 Adult (16+) and 1 Child (5-15 years) is travelling together. Maximum group size is 2 Adults and 4 Children Time Restrictions Off-Peak Day tickets may require you to travel at specific times of day, days of the week or on a specific route. Website journey planners can tell you when your ticket can be used - simply enter your outward and return journey times to see the trains you can travel on. If you board a train at a time when your Off-Peak ticket is not valid, you will be charged the difference between the fare you have paid and the cheapest valid fare for the service concerned. In some circumstances you may have to pay a penalty fare. More Information All tickets and travel are subject to the National Rail Conditions of Travel.
